Class DataCommands

java.lang.Object
server.data.DataCommands
public class DataCommands extends Object
Manages the registration and execution of commands. Implements the Singleton pattern to provide a single point of access to all available commands. Commands are stored in a HashMap with their names as keys.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static DataCommands getInstance()
      Returns the singleton instance of DataCommands.
      Returns:
      the singleton instance
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the instance has not been initialized

    • initialize

      public static void initialize()
      Initializes the singleton instance of DataCommands. This method must be called once before using the instance.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the instance has already been initialized

    • getNames

      public ArrayList<String> getNames()
      Returns a list of all registered command names.
      Returns:
      an ArrayList containing the names of all commands

    • addCommand

      public void addCommand(String commandName, Command command)
      Registers a new command with the specified name.
      Parameters:
      commandName - the name under which the command will be registered
      command - the command implementation

    • getCommand

      public Command getCommand(String commandName)
      Retrieves a command by its name.
      Parameters:
      commandName - the name of the command
      Returns:
      the Command object, or null if no command with that name exists

    • createCommandByName

      public void createCommandByName(String commandName)
      Parses and executes a command based on the input string without additional parameters. The input string may contain the command name only or with one argument.
      Parameters:
      commandName - the input string containing the command and possibly an argument

    • createCommandByName

      public void createCommandByName(String commandName, MusicBand musicBand)
      Parses and executes a command with an additional MusicBand parameter. The input string may contain the command name only or with one argument.
      Parameters:
      commandName - the input string containing the command and possibly an argument
      musicBand - the MusicBand object to pass to the command
